headlines.description: — Josh Allen says his sprained left foot felt fine — still, he was grateful for how his Buffalo teammates helped carry the load.
headlines.shortLinkText: Bills end 2-game skid with 31-14 win over spiraling Panthers

headlines.description: — Kyler Murray had nowhere to run and his passes often missed the mark.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cardinals lose chance to clinch, fall 30-12 to lowly Lions

events.name: New York Jets at Miami Dolphins

headlines.description: — Duke Johnson grew up in the shadow of Hard Rock Stadium, went to high school a couple miles away and played his college football on the Miami Dolphins' field.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dolphins extend winning streak to 6, rally past Jets 31-24

events.name: Dallas Cowboys at New York Giants

headlines.description: — It's been said defenses win championships, and nobody in the NFL might be playing better D right now than the Dallas Cowboys.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cowboys inch close to playoff berth as D, Lawrence dominate

headlines.description: — Joe Haden spent a month watching the Pittsburgh Steelers defense flail without him.
— Tremon Smith is planning to do something nice for his blockers. He doesn't know what. He doesn't know when. He just knows he needs 10 of whatever he decides to buy in the next few weeks.
headlines.shortLinkText: Texans end 3-game skid with 30-16 victory at lowly Jaguars

headlines.description: — Khalid Kareem snatched the ball  from backup quarterback Drew Lock on second-and-goal and the Cincinnati Bengals seized a first-place tie with the Baltimore Ravens atop the...
headlines.shortLinkText: Bridgewater injured in Bengals' 15-10 victory over Broncos

headlines.description: — The lackluster first two months of the season are a distant memory for the San Francisco 49ers. Heading down the stretch, the Niners have hit their stride and put themselves in prime position for a playoff spot.
headlines.shortLinkText: 49ers beat Falcons 31-13 for 5th win in 6 games

events.name: Green Bay Packers at Baltimore Ravens

headlines.description: — Aaron Rodgers tied Brett Favre for a major franchise record, and the Green Bay Packers won the NFC North.
headlines.shortLinkText: Packers clinch division after Ravens' 2-point try fails

events.name: New Orleans Saints at Tampa Bay Buccaneers

headlines.description: — Nobody hounds Tom Brady like the New Orleans Saints.
headlines.shortLinkText: Saints frustrate Brady again, beat SB champ Buccaneers 9-0
